description:
A Solar Energy Systems Engineer needs to compare the performance of a High Concentration PV (HCPV) system and a standard Flat-Plate PV system in Phoenix, Arizona.

Using either SAM's desktop GUI or a PySAM Python script, do the following:
1. Model a default HCPV system in Phoenix, AZ (use a bundled TMY weather file for Phoenix). Note its Nameplate Capacity.
2. Model a standard Flat-Plate PV system (PVWatts) in Phoenix, AZ using the EXACT SAME weather file. Configure its DC Nameplate Capacity to be exactly 1.2 times the HCPV system's capacity.
3. Run both simulations to get their Annual Energy (kWh) and Capacity Factor (%).
4. Save your work (either a .sam project file or .py script) to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/.
5. Save the comparative results as a JSON file at:
/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/technology_comparison.json

The JSON file MUST contain exactly these keys:
- weather_file (string, path to weather file)
- hcpv_system_capacity_kw (float)
- pv_system_capacity_kw (float)
- hcpv_annual_energy_kwh (float)
- pv_annual_energy_kwh (float)
- hcpv_capacity_factor (float)
- pv_capacity_factor (float)
